title: Carlyle Buys Korea Home Appliance Co. for $700 Million
summary: Carlyle Group Inc. agreed to acquire Chung Ho Group, a South Korean home and health-care appliance rental platform, for $700 million, as rising inheritance-tax burdens in the country push more owners to sell, people familiar with the matter said.

summary: Source: Bloomberg, 4:19

title: BofA Warns It’s Time to ‘Take Profits’ as Red Flags Multiply
summary: Investors should exercise caution regarding US stocks as an increasing number of “bear market signposts” point to an approaching top, according to Bank of America Securities.

title: Treasuries Pare Drop in Oil-Driven Move Tied to Iran War
summary: Treasuries pared declines, pulling two-year yields back from a more than 15-month high, as reports of a ceasefire between Israel and Iran eased the upward pressure on oil prices.

title: Amazon to Raise C$14 Billion in Record Canadian Dollar Bond Sale
summary: Amazon.com Inc. is set to raise C$14 billion ($10 billion) from investment-grade bonds in Canadian dollars, the largest such offering on record, as US hyperscalers look beyond their home market to fund artificial intelligence spending.

title: Chip Stocks Poised for Best Day in Year After Rout: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, lifting stocks amid renewed enthusiasm over artificial intelligence and bets that a solid economy will keep powering Corporate America.

title: Mercuria’s First-Half Profit Jumped 88% on Commodity Shocks
summary: Mercuria Energy Group enjoyed an 88% jump in first-half profit, putting it on track for one of its best-ever annual results as the Hormuz crisis creates huge opportunities for commodity traders.

title: Buyer Flips London Mega Mansion for £56 Million Mark Up
summary: The mystery buyer who acquired a mansion in London’s Regent’s Park at the end of 2024 has sold the property for about £195 million ($260 million) in one of the UK’s highest value sales of all time.

title: Dollar Is Best Bet in New Global Regime of High Rates, BMO Says
summary: Betting on continued US dollar strength is the cleanest way for foreign-exchange traders to take a position on a new regime of higher rates and inflation, according to BMO Capital Markets.

title: Strategy Resumes Buying Bitcoin After Rare Sale Roils Crypto
summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. resumed buying Bitcoin a week after a rare token sale by the digital asset treasury company roiled the crypto market.

title: Blue Owl Fund Hit by Redemptions Set to Sell $500 Million Bond
summary: One of Blue Owl Capital Inc.’s private credit funds that capped redemptions earlier this year is set to raise $500 million from an investment-grade bond sale.

title: Giant Oil Supertanker Orders Eclipse Record Set in 2008
summary: The world’s shipowners have placed orders for a record number of new oil supertankers, surpassing a boom back in 2008 that ultimately led to a glut and a collapse in rates.

title: Drahi Reaches €20 Billion Deal to Sell Phone Carrier SFR
summary: Billionaire Patrick Drahi has reached an agreement to sell French mobile carrier SFR to a consortium of rival telecommunications firms, in a deal that values the company €20.4 billion ($23.5 billion) and will test regulators’ tolerance for consolidation.

title: Private Share Marketplace Hiive Shops Equity as IPO Boom Nears
summary: The Hiive Co., an online platform for trading shares in pre-public companies, recently held talks with investors about a secondary stock sale that would have valued the firm at about $780 million, according to a document seen by Bloomberg.

title: SpaceX IPO Fuels Space Economy Buzz
summary: SpaceX is just days from Wall Street's biggest IPO ever. And as capital pours into the Space Economy, the opportunities go beyond rocketships. Space exploration yields critical infrastructure for plenty of Earth-bound industries, like agriculture and drug research. Ross Hamilton, the COO of Space Network and the author of "Everything is Flying" joined Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about how space technology already runs modern life on Earth and why a SpaceX IPO could reshape far more than the space industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bitcoin’s $235 Billion Crash Masks a Bigger Shift Across Crypto
summary: The easiest way to understand crypto used to be to look at Bitcoin.

title: A 20-Year Real Estate “Super Cycle” Is Beginning: Rabil
summary: Kayne Anderson CEO Al Rabil joins Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why he believes housing, medical offices, senior housing, and industrial real estate are entering a 20-year super cycle. He argues supply constraints, aging demographics, and AI-driven efficiencies will create powerful tailwinds, while operational expertise, not just capital, will separate winners from the competition. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: European Stocks Fall on Mideast Tensions; Zealand Pharma Sinks
summary: European stocks fell after a choppy session as investors reacted to conflicting signals about the war in the Middle East.

title: Stocks Gain as Buyers Step In After AI-Led Selloff
summary:
Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, lifting stocks amid renewed enthusiasm over artificial intelligence and bets that a solid economy will keep powering Corporate America.
Chris Harvey, Head of Equity & Portfolio Strategy at CIBC Capital Markets, discusses signs of a potential market melt-up. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rising Dollar Swings Threaten to Upend Resilient Carry Trades
summary: Rising dollar volatility is endangering a currency-trading strategy that had been a steady winner in recent months.

title: US Stocks Rebound From Selloff as Chipmakers Lead Broad Gains
summary: US stocks advanced on Monday, paring last week’s slump, as chipmakers headed for their best session in a year and traders assessed flaring tensions in the Middle East, which supported oil prices and energy shares.

title: Apple Investors Look for AI Overhaul to Power Next Leg of Gains
summary: Apple Inc. investors have spent nearly two years clamoring for the iPhone maker to make a big splash with artificial intelligence. Their wait may finally be coming to an end this week at the company’s annual Worldwide Developers Conference.

title: US Steel Sees Pennsylvania Overhaul Costing Up to $2.5 Billion
summary: US Steel said investments to upgrade its Mon Valley operations in Pennsylvania could total as much as $2.5 billion, more than double Nippon Steel Corp.’s minimum commitment as part of its takeover offer for the American company.

title: United Airlines CEO on Demand, Consumers, Oil Prices
summary: Despite worries in other regions of the world, United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby says airline demand and capacity in the US remains strong, and consumers remain resilient despite some fare increases driven by the spike in oil prices caused by the conflict in Iran. Kirby spoke to Lisa Abramowicz at IATA in Rio de Janeiro on June 7, 2026. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Air France Could Consider Being Part of EasyJet Bid, CEO Says
summary: In an interview at the IATA annual meeting in Rio de Janeiro, Air France-KLM CEO Benjamin Smith said the airline may need to raise ticket prices to compensate for rising fuel costs sparked by war in the Middle East. The carrier is also looking to reopen routes to the region when  it's safer. Smith signaled he’d be willing to consider becoming involved in a bid for EasyJet Plc.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Remittances Lift India’s Current Account, Cushion War Shock
summary: India recorded a surprise current account surplus in the January-March period as strong services receipts and higher remittances offset disruptions caused by the Iran war.

title: GE Aerospace CEO Is Optimistic for More China Deals
summary: In an interview at the IATA annual meeting in Rio de Janeiro, GE Aerospace CEO Larry Culp remained optimistic that GE could secure more aircraft-engine orders from China following a meeting between President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping in Beijing the previous month, after which their manufacturing partner, Boeing Co. secured an initial batch of commitments. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Etihad Airways CEO Says Carrier Is Discounting Tickets
summary: Etihad Airways CEO Antonoaldo Neves speaks to Bloomberg's Guy Johnson at the International Air Transport Association (IATA) conference in Rio de Janeiro. He says the carrier is discounting tickets by keeping fares at a stable level despite the 70% jump in fuel costs. Neves also talks about securing delivery dates for new aircraft from Boeing and Airbus SE. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Campbell's Earnings Beat; Eli Lilly Obesity Drug Results | Stock Movers
summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Shares of The Campbell's Company (CPB) edged higher ahead of the US market open after the canned soup maker reported adjusted earnings per share for the third quarter that beat the average analyst estimate.
- Eli Lilly (LLY) shares gained in the early session following obesity drug presentations at the American Diabetes Association conference. Citi analysts say their conviction on Lilly is firmly intact, given the company’s incretin portfolio is “not built around singular blockbusters.” 
- Shares of Marvell Technology (MRVL) and Flex (FLEX) are rising in premarket trading as the companies are set to replace Pool Corp. and Campbell’s in S&P 500 before the market open on June 22, S&P Dow Jones Indices says in emailed statement. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bloomberg Surveillance 6/8/2026
summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)
